framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Public IC die imaging and silicon reverse engineering
paths.what_it_is: Delid, image, and analyze the internal structure of an integrated circuit, then publish a detailed write-up or high-resolution die photography explaining how the silicon actually works.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can read silicon at the transistor and process-node level, a rare depth of understanding that goes far beyond datasheet-level knowledge.

paths.what_it_is: Design a hardware product in the open, publish its schematics, PCB layouts, and firmware, and sustain it through community adoption and iteration.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can run the full engineering loop - design, manufacturing, documentation, support - in public, where the work can be independently scrutinized and reused.

paths.path: Rigorous public teardown and board-level reverse engineering
paths.what_it_is: Publicly trace a schematic from a bare board, diagnose failures at the component level, and document the reverse-engineered circuit behavior.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves fast, disciplined circuit-level diagnosis under real-world constraints - the skill that separates engineers who can debug a misbehaving board from those who can only design one in isolation.
